top_stories.label: Top Stories
top_stories.ai_summary: Venezuela's earthquake disaster is worsening: the official toll from Wednesday's twin magnitude-7.2 and 7.5 quakes has jumped to at least 164 dead and more than 900 injured as the count expands to include hard-hit La Guaira, with the US, China and India offering aid. Western Europe's record heatwave is shifting east and France has raised its health alert to the highest level. On Iran, the war deal is holding - oil has fallen back to pre-war levels as Hormuz traffic rebounds, while Secretary of State Rubio courts Gulf states and warns against any Iranian tolls, and Trump asked Congress for about US$88 billion in extra funding. In tech, Anthropic accused Alibaba of the largest known 'distillation' attack on its Claude models.

stories.headline: Venezuela twin earthquakes kill at least 164, injure more than 900
stories.summary: The death toll from Wednesday's back-to-back earthquakes in Venezuela - a magnitude-7.2 quake about 160km west of Caracas followed less than a minute later by a magnitude-7.5, per the USGS - has climbed to at least 164 dead and more than 900 injured, interim president Delcy Rodriguez said, after an earlier count of 32 dead omitted the hard-hit coastal state of La Guaira. It is the country's most powerful quake since 1900; the US, China and India have offered to send help as rescuers dig through collapsed buildings.

stories.headline: Magnitude-7.2 quake strikes off northern Japan; no major damage
stories.summary: A magnitude-7.2 earthquake struck off Iwate in northern Japan on June 25, Japan's Meteorological Agency said, but officials reported no major damage and no destructive tsunami. The jolt came hours after the deadly Venezuela quakes, an unusually active day for large seismic events.

stories.headline: Europe's record heatwave shifts east as France raises health alert to highest level
stories.summary: France raised its health alert to the highest level as a record-breaking heatwave gripping western Europe began shifting east, after Britain logged its hottest June day and France earlier reported its hottest day ever. The heat has triggered health warnings, power strain and livestock deaths, and reignited a French political row over air-conditioning.

stories.headline: Oil prices fall back to pre-war levels as Hormuz traffic rebounds
stories.summary: Oil prices fell back to levels seen before the US-Iran war, with Brent sliding as shipping through the Strait of Hormuz rebounds and Middle East supply rises under the war-ending deal. The drop eases a key inflation worry even as the US works to reassure Gulf allies about the agreement.

stories.headline: Trump asks Congress for about US$88 billion in extra funding, mostly for Iran war
stories.summary: President Trump asked Congress for roughly US$88 billion in additional funding, the bulk of it to cover what the White House called 'urgent' costs of the Iran war, despite tension with some Republicans over the request. It lands as Washington works to implement the war-ending deal with Tehran.

stories.headline: Rubio courts Gulf states over Iran deal, warns against Hormuz tolls
stories.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio toured Gulf capitals seeking backing for the deal ending the US-Iran war, telling allies Washington wants an agreement with Tehran but 'not at any price' and warning that any Iranian tolls on Strait of Hormuz transits would 'spread like contagion' to other waterways. The UN nuclear watchdog says inspectors will visit Iranian sites under the deal.

stories.headline: Anthropic accuses Alibaba of largest known 'distillation' attack on Claude models
stories.summary: Anthropic told a US Senate committee that operators it links to Alibaba's Qwen AI lab ran some 28.8 million exchanges with its models using around 25,000 fraudulent accounts between April 22 and June 5, in what it called the largest known 'adversarial distillation' attack on its Claude models - an attempt to cheaply mimic Claude's coding and agentic capabilities. The claim is an accusation by Anthropic; Alibaba had not publicly responded in detail.

stories.headline: Asian chip stocks rally on strong Micron forecast as Wall Street slips
stories.summary: Asian technology shares rallied, with South Korea's KOSPI jumping after a blowout Micron forecast lifted chipmakers across the region - a sharp turn from the prior session's sell-off. The bounce came even as Wall Street's Nasdaq fell for a third straight day.

singapore.label: Singapore
singapore.ai_summary: Cost-of-living and cash access led the day: DPM Gan said Singapore is exploring PayNow upgrades and NETS QR integration, and authorities pledged an ATM, cashpoint or bank branch within 500m of every HDB block by end-2027. Politics stirred too, with reports of unhappy Workers' Party cadres preparing to challenge Pritam Singh's leadership. In the courts, a firm was fined S$500,000 and a director jailed over the 2021 Tuas blast that killed three, a former PR was fined S$9,000 for defaulting on national service, and the unpaid-wages saga widened to around 400 workers. NDP 2026 will field its first-ever contingent of persons with disabilities. On the lighter side, the Teochew film Dear You won 50 extra screenings and a 12-year-old's mee hoon kueh promo went viral. Expect thundery showers, 26-34C.

stories.headline: Singapore exploring PayNow upgrades and NETS QR integration: DPM Gan
stories.summary: Singapore is exploring enhancements to PayNow, including reducing the number of payment steps and integrating it with NETS QR, Deputy Prime Minister Gan Kim Yong said, as the country pushes to make digital payments faster and more seamless for users and merchants.

stories.headline: Every HDB block to have an ATM, cashpoint or bank branch within 500m by end-2027
stories.summary: Every HDB block will have an ATM, cashpoint or bank branch within 500m by the end of 2027, the authorities said, part of a push to keep cash accessible - particularly for seniors - even as Singapore goes increasingly cashless.

stories.headline: Will Pritam Singh stay or be ousted as WP chief? Unhappy cadres plan a fight
stories.summary: Some Workers' Party cadres unhappy with the party's direction are preparing to put up a fight over its leadership ahead of internal elections, raising questions over whether Pritam Singh stays on as secretary-general, The Straits Times reported. The WP has not announced any change to its leadership.

stories.headline: Tuas fatal blast: firm fined S$500k, director jailed over 2021 explosion that killed 3
stories.summary: A company was fined S$500,000 and one of its directors jailed more than 18 months over the 2021 Tuas explosion that killed three workers, concluding a case stemming from safety lapses at the premises.

stories.headline: Former PR fined S$9,000 for defaulting on national service
stories.summary: A former permanent resident who defaulted on national service was fined S$9,000 and has lost his PR status, the authorities said, in the latest case underscoring Singapore's tough stance on NS defaulters.

stories.headline: Malaysia-registered taxis to pay S$15 per Singapore trip from 2027
stories.summary: From 2027, Malaysia-registered taxis entering Singapore will pay a S$15 fee per trip, up from S$2 a month currently, the Land Transport Authority said, as it overhauls the cross-border taxi permit scheme.

stories.headline: Unpaid-wages case: KPA Engineering showed financial woes since 2023; about 400 workers affected
stories.summary: KPA Engineering, one of three firms at the centre of a widening unpaid-wages case affecting about 400 migrant workers, had shown signs of financial trouble since 2023 and still owes DBS money, The Straits Times reported. All three firms share a director who is a Singapore PR and has left the country; NTUC has lined up job openings and relief payments for affected workers.

stories.headline: NDP 2026 to feature first-ever persons-with-disabilities contingent
stories.summary: This year's National Day Parade will feature its first-ever civilian contingent of persons with disabilities, organisers said, in a move to make the national celebration more inclusive.

stories.headline: Nearly 4 in 5 Singapore adults with anxiety or depression don't seek help: Duke-NUS study
stories.summary: Nearly four in five Singapore adults experiencing anxiety or depression do not seek professional help, a Duke-NUS study found, with cost, stigma and a tendency to self-manage among the barriers. Researchers urged easier access to mental-health support.

stories.headline: 'I'm too famous already': 12-year-old goes viral promoting mum's mee hoon kueh stall
stories.summary: A 12-year-old boy went viral after a cheeky clip of him promoting his mother's mee hoon kueh stall - declaring 'I'm too famous already' - charmed Singaporeans online and drew a crowd of curious diners to the Greenview stall.

stories.headline: Teochew film 'Dear You' gets 50 extra screenings as dialect debate grows
stories.summary: Singapore's IMDA approved 50 additional screenings of the popular Chinese film Dear You in Teochew, after strong demand for dialect screenings reignited a public conversation about the place of Chinese dialects in Singapore.

stories.headline: Singapore bedrooms average a muggy 28C at night, new study finds
stories.summary: The average night-time temperature in Singapore bedrooms is a muggy 28 deg C, a new study of residents found, raising questions about sleep, comfort and energy use as the country warms. Researchers said the findings could inform housing design and cooling strategies.

wars.label: Wars & Conflicts
wars.ai_summary: The US-Iran war deal is holding and the markets are showing it: oil has fallen back to pre-war levels as Strait of Hormuz traffic rebounds, while Secretary of State Rubio tours the Gulf seeking backing - telling allies Washington wants a deal 'not at any price' and warning against any Iranian tolls on Hormuz - and Trump asks Congress for about US$88 billion in mostly war-related funding. In Lebanon, a fragile Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire remains strained: Israel's defence minister insists there is 'no US demand' to withdraw, and displaced residents say the truce has brought no relief. On the Russia-Ukraine front, Russia struck Ukrainian locomotives and fuel stations, killing one, after Ukrainian drones knocked out power in Russian-held Sevastopol and Zelensky vowed preemptive strikes on Russian war infrastructure.

theatres.name: US/Israel-Iran (Strait of Hormuz)
theatres.status: War-ending deal holding and moving to implementation; oil back to pre-war levels; Hormuz reopened to shipping; US seeking Gulf backing and extra war funding

timeline.event: US and Iranian presidents sign a deal aiming to end the war.
First round of US-Iran implementation talks ends with what mediators call encouraging progress.

The IAEA chief says inspectors will visit Iranian sites under the deal; dozens of ships transit the Strait of Hormuz under a UN IMO scheme to evacuate stranded vessels. Iran says it has made no new nuclear commitments.

Oil prices fall back to pre-war levels as Hormuz traffic rebounds; Secretary of State Rubio tours the Gulf seeking backing for the deal, says the US wants a deal 'not at any price' and warns any Iranian tolls on Hormuz would 'spread like contagion'; Trump asks Congress for about US$88 billion in extra funding, mostly for Iran war costs.

theatres.name: Israel-Lebanon / Gaza
theatres.status: Fragile Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire (agreed June 19) repeatedly strained; withdrawal disputed; displaced residents say truce brings no relief

timeline.event: Israel and Hezbollah agree a ceasefire, the US says, after Lebanon reported Israeli strikes killing 47 and Israel said four soldiers were killed by Hezbollah.
Israeli troops kill two in south Lebanon after a lull, local authorities say; Hezbollah's chief demands a full, timetabled Israeli withdrawal and new Lebanon-Israel talks are announced.

Israel's defence minister says there is 'no US demand' for Israel to withdraw from Lebanon, while residents displaced from destroyed and Israeli-occupied border towns say the truce has brought them no relief - underscoring how shaky the ceasefire remains.

theatres.name: Russia-Ukraine War
theatres.status: Russia presses on with strikes on Ukrainian rail and fuel infrastructure; Ukraine signalling preemptive strikes after hitting Crimea; diplomacy stalled

timeline.event: A Ukrainian drone attack on Russian-occupied Crimea knocks out power in Sevastopol, the peninsula's largest city, after striking its main substation; Kyiv has been intensifying attacks on energy facilities to dent Russian oil revenues.
Russia strikes Ukrainian locomotives and fuel stations, killing one person, an official says, days after President Zelensky vowed preemptive attacks on facilities Russia uses to wage the war.

outbreaks.label: Outbreaks
outbreaks.ai_summary: The Bundibugyo-strain Ebola outbreak in DR Congo and Uganda remains the dominant global health threat: WHO's latest Disease Outbreak News (June 19) counted 915 confirmed cases and 234 deaths, concentrated in DR Congo's Ituri province, after France confirmed the first-ever case in Europe (a returning doctor, now stable). WHO's chief says the global risk 'remains low', and Trump has sought more than US$1.4 billion in Ebola funding. WHO also flagged rising yellow fever transmission, notably in the Americas. On the brighter side, the MV Hondius cruise-ship hantavirus outbreak is winding down - WHO expects to formally declare it over on July 2.

outbreaks.name: Ebola (Bundibugyo virus) - DR Congo & Uganda
outbreaks.status: Escalating; outbreak concentrated in DRC's Ituri province; first case exported to Europe
outbreaks.scope_note: WHO DON608 (published 19 Jun, figures as of 17-18 Jun), DRC + Uganda combined: DRC 896 cases / 232 deaths (CFR ~26%), Uganda 19 cases / 2 deaths; 88 recovered. Ituri Province accounts for ~91% of confirmed cases. France confirmed 1 imported case on 24 Jun (doctor, stable) - the first outside Africa; 'countries' counts DRC, Uganda and France (1 imported case). Newer wire/ministry tallies running ahead of the DON are not yet WHO-ratified.

outbreaks.name: MV Hondius - Andes hantavirus (cruise ship)
outbreaks.status: Resolving; no new cases; WHO expects to formally declare the outbreak over on 2 July
outbreaks.scope_note: WHO DON604 (27 May): 11 confirmed + 2 probable cases and 2 confirmed + 1 probable deaths among passengers/crew of the Dutch expedition ship MV Hondius; 600+ contacts traced across 32 countries/territories. Figures have been stable since late May; CDC's 42-day monitoring of exposed US travellers ended 21 Jun, and WHO expects to formally declare the outbreak over on 2 Jul 2026.

stories.headline: France confirms first Ebola case outside Africa in current outbreak
stories.summary: France confirmed its first-ever Ebola case on June 24 - a doctor who returned from DR Congo, now in stable condition in a specialised facility. It is the first confirmed case outside Africa in the current outbreak; the health ministry said the public risk was very low and was tracing contacts. The outbreak is caused by the Bundibugyo species, for which there is no licensed vaccine.

stories.headline: WHO chief says global Ebola risk 'remains low' despite first case in Europe
stories.summary: After France detected the first Ebola case outside Africa in the current outbreak, the WHO chief said the global risk remains low, citing established containment and contact-tracing measures. WHO continues to assess the risk inside DR Congo as very high given ongoing transmission and spread into new health zones.

stories.headline: Trump seeks more than US$1.4 billion in Ebola funding from Congress
stories.summary: President Trump asked Congress for more than US$1.4 billion to fund the response to the Bundibugyo-virus Ebola outbreak, as US authorities warn it could become one of the largest Ebola outbreaks on record. The request came a day after France reported the first case outside Africa.

stories.headline: WHO reports rapid rise in DR Congo-Uganda Ebola outbreak
stories.summary: In its June 19 Disease Outbreak News, WHO reported 915 confirmed Bundibugyo-virus cases (896 in DR Congo, 19 in Uganda) and 234 deaths, with 220 additional cases and 96 deaths reported in DR Congo since June 13 - partly due to expanded testing. Ituri province accounts for more than 90% of confirmed infections, and conflict in eastern DR Congo is hampering the response.

stories.headline: WHO: cruise-ship hantavirus outbreak should formally end on July 2
stories.summary: WHO said the Andes hantavirus outbreak linked to the Dutch expedition cruise ship MV Hondius should be formally declared over on July 2, after no new cases and the close of the monitoring window; the US CDC said the 42-day monitoring of exposed American travellers ended on June 21. The outbreak infected 13 people (3 deaths) and prompted contact-tracing across 32 countries and territories.

stories.headline: WHO flags rising yellow fever transmission, notably in the Americas
stories.summary: In a fresh global Disease Outbreak News on June 24, WHO said sylvatic yellow fever transmission has been sustained in Africa and is notably rising in the Americas, including spread into lower-risk zones. WHO estimates yellow fever causes roughly 67,000-173,000 severe cases and 31,000-82,000 deaths globally each year, and urged vaccination in high-risk countries where coverage lags.

cybersecurity.label: CyberSecurity
cybersecurity.ai_summary: Actively exploited enterprise flaws dominate this cycle. Mandiant revealed a Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN bug (CVE-2026-20245, CVSS 7.8) was exploited as a zero-day to gain root, while CISA flagged a critical, exploited code-injection flaw in Lantronix EDS5000 devices (CVE-2025-67038, CVSS 9.8) and max-severity Ubiquiti UniFi OS flaws (CVSS 10.0) now under attack; a Cisco Unified CM bug (CVE-2026-20230, CVSS 8.6) is also being exploited after a public PoC. AI is increasingly in the crosshairs - new 'Gaslight' macOS malware uses prompt injection to disrupt AI-assisted analysis. Supply-chain and breach activity stayed heavy: Cordyceps CI/CD flaws exposed 300+ GitHub repos, malicious npm and WordPress plugins spread, and Tata Electronics and healthtech firm Xolis (1.4 million people) disclosed breaches. Law enforcement landed blows too, with Operation Endgame disrupting the Amadey and StealC networks and recovering 27 million credentials.

stories.headline: New 'Gaslight' macOS malware uses prompt injection to disrupt AI-assisted analysis
stories.summary: Researchers detailed 'Gaslight', new macOS malware that embeds prompt-injection instructions designed to mislead the AI tools defenders increasingly use to triage and analyse samples - an emerging tactic that targets the AI in the security workflow itself rather than the host alone.

stories.headline: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN zero-day (CVE-2026-20245) exploited to gain root access
stories.summary: Mandiant found an unknown threat actor exploited a Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager flaw (CVE-2026-20245, CVSS 7.8) as a zero-day - at least two months before public disclosure - to gain root access on targeted systems. The bug is in CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog (added June 9); administrators are urged to patch and review SD-WAN exposure.
stories.source: BleepingComputer, The Hacker News, CISA, NVD

stories.headline: Malicious Edge extension abuses Native Messaging as a bridge to malware
stories.summary: Researchers found a malicious Microsoft Edge extension abusing the browser's Native Messaging API to act as a bridge to local malware, letting attackers escalate from the browser to the host. Users are advised to audit installed extensions and restrict native-messaging hosts.

stories.headline: CISA warns critical Lantronix EDS5000 flaw is being actively exploited
stories.summary: CISA warned of active exploitation of a critical command-injection flaw in Lantronix EDS5000 Series devices (CVE-2025-67038, CVSS 9.8), where the HTTP RPC module runs a shell command on failed authentication using attacker-controlled input. The flaw was added to CISA's KEV catalog on June 23; federal agencies were ordered to apply fixes by June 26, 2026.

stories.headline: Amadey and StealC malware networks disrupted in Operation Endgame; 27M credentials recovered
stories.summary: Law enforcement disrupted the Amadey and StealC malware operations as part of Operation Endgame, seizing infrastructure and recovering some 27 million stolen credentials. The action targets two prolific loaders/infostealers used to feed initial access to other criminal operations.

stories.headline: CISA warns of max-severity Ubiquiti UniFi OS flaws exploited in attacks
stories.summary: CISA warned that maximum-severity Ubiquiti UniFi OS flaws are being exploited, including a command-injection bug (CVE-2026-34910) and an improper-access-control bug (CVE-2026-34908), both rated CVSS 10.0, plus a path-traversal flaw (CVE-2026-34909). A network-adjacent attacker could execute commands or make unauthorised system changes; all three were added to the KEV catalog on June 23 and admins are urged to patch immediately.

stories.headline: Cordyceps CI/CD flaws expose 300+ GitHub repositories to supply-chain attacks
stories.summary: Researchers disclosed 'Cordyceps', a set of CI/CD pipeline flaws that exposed more than 300 GitHub repositories to supply-chain attacks via abusable workflow patterns. GitHub has since updated its widely used actions/checkout to block common 'pwn request' attack patterns.

stories.headline: Stealthy 'Mistic' backdoor linked to ransomware access broker KongTuke
stories.summary: Researchers detailed a stealthy backdoor dubbed 'Mistic', tied to KongTuke - an access broker that sells initial network footholds to ransomware operators - and observed in ClickFix and ModeloRAT campaigns, making infections an early warning of a potential ransomware attack.

stories.headline: DoJ seizes Huione cloud account tied to cyber-scam money laundering
stories.summary: The US Justice Department seized a cloud account linked to Huione, a platform tied to laundering proceeds from cyber-scam operations, in the latest action against the financial infrastructure underpinning South-east Asian 'pig-butchering' fraud networks.

stories.headline: Cisco Unified CM flaw CVE-2026-20230 now exploited in attacks
stories.summary: A high-severity flaw in Cisco Unified Communications Manager and its Session Management Edition (CVE-2026-20230, CVSS 8.6) is now being exploited after a published proof-of-concept revealed a file-write path leading to root, allowing an unauthenticated remote attacker to compromise affected systems. Cisco customers are urged to patch.

stories.headline: Tata Electronics confirms cyberattack as hackers leak data
stories.summary: India's Tata Electronics confirmed a cyberattack after threat actors began leaking stolen data, with the attackers claiming to expose Apple and Tesla trade secrets. The company said it was investigating the scope of the breach.

stories.headline: Healthtech firm Xolis breach exposes data of 1.4 million people
stories.summary: Healthtech company Xolis disclosed a data breach affecting around 1.4 million people, exposing personal and potentially health-related information - a high-impact incident given the sensitivity of medical data.

stories.headline: New macOS 'ClickFix' attack silently mounts DMGs to push infostealer
stories.summary: A new macOS ClickFix-style attack silently mounts disk images (DMGs) to deliver an infostealer, tricking users into running malicious commands and broadening the social-engineering technique beyond Windows.

stories.headline: 'FortiBleed' campaign harvested credentials from FortiGate firewalls at scale
stories.summary: The 'FortiBleed' campaign used a custom FortiGate sniffer to steal credentials from internet-facing firewalls in what reports described as a 110-million-credential harvesting operation; CISA has warned Fortinet customers. Administrators are urged to rotate credentials and review exposed appliances.

stories.headline: Scattered Spider members plead guilty to hacking Transport for London
stories.summary: Members of the Scattered Spider cybercrime group pleaded guilty to hacking Transport for London, a notable accountability milestone against a prolific extortion-focused threat collective.

stories.headline: Fake AI-agent 'skill' passed security scans and reached 26,000 agents
stories.summary: Researchers reported that a malicious AI-agent 'skill' passed automated security scans and was reportedly installed on around 26,000 agents, highlighting the emerging supply-chain risk in agentic-AI ecosystems where add-ons run with broad permissions.

stories.headline: Malicious npm packages pose as PostCSS tools to deliver Windows RAT
stories.summary: Attackers published malicious npm packages masquerading as PostCSS tooling to deliver a Windows remote-access trojan, the latest in a steady stream of open-source registry supply-chain poisoning targeting developers.

stories.headline: WhatsApp campaign uses fake business documents to install RMM tool
stories.summary: A phishing campaign abuses WhatsApp and fake business documents with VBScript to install the ManageEngine remote monitoring and management (RMM) tool, giving attackers hands-on control of victim PCs.

stories.headline: ShapedPlugin WordPress Pro plugins backdoored in supply-chain attack
stories.summary: Pro plugins from ShapedPlugin were backdoored in a supply-chain attack, potentially giving attackers access to WordPress sites that installed the trojanised versions. Affected site owners are urged to audit and update.

stories.headline: 29-year-old Squid proxy bug 'Squidbleed' can leak cleartext HTTP requests
stories.summary: Researchers disclosed 'Squidbleed', a 29-year-old bug in the Squid caching proxy that can leak cleartext HTTP requests, potentially exposing sensitive data passing through affected proxies.

stories.headline: 'DifyTap' flaws in Dify could expose AI chats across tenants
stories.summary: Researchers detailed 'DifyTap' flaws in the Dify AI application platform that could expose users' AI chats across different tenants, a multi-tenancy isolation failure with privacy implications for organisations building on the platform.
